title: erythraea tenuifolia subsp. conferta
intro: Erythraea tenuifolia subsp. conferta, also known as the wood rose or wild rose, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Erythraea tenuifolia subsp. conferta include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties.
